openingMessage: I don't hire good coaches. I hire good people. If they turn out to be good coaches too, that's a plus.
openingWhy: You can teach a man to run a 4-gas in a week. You can't teach him to tell the truth about a reading that inconveniences the schedule. Character first — the skills follow. It's the same reason the best crews in this Basin aren't always the most experienced ones.
leadershipThought: Journey management is not paperwork for long hauls. It's somebody knowing where you are, what route you're on, and when to worry. On a lease road at 2 AM, that call-in is your attendant.

Exclusion Zone Breach — Third-Party Personnel (Near Miss) (Line of Fire)
When SimOps create unplanned traffic near your exclusion zone, proactive interception by all crew members — not just the safety tech — is what prevents a line-of-fire exposure.

fieldNews.headline: SM Energy #112 — 6,000 ppm H2S / 2% CO2 (carry-forward from 7/10)
fieldNews.detail: Michael Hutt reported it Friday. Confirm current readings before any entry or first break this week.

fieldNews.headline: Two driving near-misses flagged Friday — stay heads-up on the road
fieldNews.detail: Jordan Lemmons (swerving driver, Jal 128) and Alex Clubb (service truck into his lane at 75 mph). The drive is part of the job.

todaysChallenge: If the reading looks off, test again. If the form's wrong, redo it. 'Close enough' isn't a Canon phrase — stay on it until it's right.
todaysChallengeSubtext: “Champions keep playing until they get it right.” — Billie Jean King
